notes: Library card sections completed for parent choice: reading, vocabulary, journals, writing, questions, informational text, and assessments.
Structured from existing markdown unit card.
This is currently assigned to the 4th grade Unit 2 pocket because the local 4th grade file set does not include Treasure Island yet.

book.title: The Three Musketeers
book.author: Alexandre Dumas
book.description: Author: Alexandre Dumas Grade Level: 4th Grade (Ages 9-10) Unit Length: 30 lessons (24 regular + 6 assessments) Days: 31-60 (Weeks 7-12) - UNIT 2 Theme: Friendship, Loyalty, Courage, Adventure Reading Level: Classic adventure, age-appropriate adaptation This card now includes complete weekly library sections for Grade 4 use.

weeks.title: Week 7: Meeting the Musketeers (Days 31-35)
reading: Chapters 1-4: The Boy from Gascony, Trouble in Meung, Three Duels Before Noon, All for One
journal: What does this week's reading show about Friendship? Use one scene as evidence.
Choose one character decision from Chapters 1-4: The Boy from Gascony, Trouble in Meung, Three Duels Before Noon, All for One. Would you make the same choice? Explain why.
writing: Write an ACE paragraph answering one comprehension question with text evidence.
Draft a short narrative response connected to this week's reading.
questions: What problem or conflict matters most in Chapters 1-4: The Boy from Gascony, Trouble in Meung, Three Duels Before Noon, All for One?
Which detail best reveals a character trait, motivation, or change?
How does the setting affect what happens in this section?
What lesson, theme, or big idea is starting to develop?
assessment: Weekly assessment or review day included in source card.
